card.intuition: Measuring the performance of your code to identify bottlenecks (Profiling) and comparing different implementations to find the fastest one (Benchmarking).
card.analogy: Profiling is like looking at a heart rate monitor while you run to see exactly when you get tired. Benchmarking is like running a 100m sprint against a friend to see who is faster.
when_to_use: Finding exactly which function is making your API slow
Choosing between two different libraries for JSON parsing
Measuring the impact of a new database index on query speed
gotchas: Don't optimize without profiling first — you might waste time fixing a function that only takes 1% of the total execution time
Micro-benchmarks (measuring a single line of code) can be misleading because compilers and CPUs perform various optimizations that don't happen in 'real' code

steps.label: Baseline
steps.annotation: Establish baseline metrics before optimising — never guess.

steps.label: Profile
steps.annotation: CPU profiler shows hot functions. Heap snapshot shows allocations.

steps.label: Identify
steps.annotation: Find the bottleneck: 80% of time in 20% of code.

steps.label: Optimise
steps.annotation: Optimise only the hot path. Premature optimisation wastes time.

steps.label: Verify
steps.annotation: Re-run benchmark to confirm improvement. Compare before/after numbers.

exercise.prompt: Identify the type of chart often used in profiling to visualize the call stack and time spent in functions.
hints: It looks like a fire because the most 'active' functions are at the top
exercise.test_code:
assert identify_chart("Visualization of stack traces over time") == "Flame Graph"
print("PASS")
